message:
Fix remaining cass→cm command naming in beads

Fixed wrong 'cass' patterns that should be 'cm' (cass-memory):
- cass forget → cm forget (2 instances)
- cass learn → cm learn (2 instances)
- cass context → cm context (2 instances)
- cass reflect → cm reflect (1 instance)
- cass status → cm doctor (1 instance)
- cass mark → cm mark (2 instances)
- cass block → cm block (1 instance)
- cass archive → cm archive (1 instance)

Verified correct 'cass' patterns remain (referring to cass session search tool):
- cass health (3 instances)
- cass search (30 instances)
- cass index (17 instances)
- cass stats (2 instances)
- cass rebuild (2 instances)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -174,7 +174,7 @@
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-gp8","title":"[Reflector] shouldExitEarly - Determine if more iterations needed","description":"function shouldExitEarly(
  iteration: number,
  deltasThisIteration: number,
  totalDeltas: number,
  config: Config
): boolean

Exit early if:
- No new deltas this iteration
- Already have max deltas (20)
- Iteration >= maxReflectorIterations

Saves LLM calls when early iterations capture everything

LOGIC:
```typescript
if (deltasThisIteration === 0) return true
if (totalDeltas >= 20) return true
if (iteration >= config.maxReflectorIterations - 1) return true
return false
```

WHY EARLY EXIT:
1. No new deltas: LLM has exhausted insights
2. Max deltas (20): diminishing returns, avoid overwhelming playbook
3. Max iterations: hard limit to prevent infinite loops

EXAMPLE SCENARIOS:
- Iteration 0: 15 deltas → continue
- Iteration 1: 0 deltas → exit (total: 15)
- Iteration 0: 12 deltas → continue
- Iteration 1: 8 deltas → exit (total: 20, hit max)
- Iteration 2: 3 deltas → continue
- Iteration 3: 2 deltas → exit (hit maxReflectorIterations)

CONFIG:
- maxReflectorIterations: default 3
- maxDeltas: hardcoded 20

OPTIMIZATION:
Early exit saves unnecessary LLM calls and reduces latency

Used in reflectOnSession() main loop","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:03.662093-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:04.371872-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-gr56","title":"[CLI] audit command - Check for rule violations","description":"Scan recent sessions for playbook rule violations.

Command signature:
```bash
cass-memory audit [options]

Options:
  --days N           Scan sessions from last N days (default: 7)
  --workspace PATH   Filter by workspace
  --json             Output as JSON
```

Purpose:
Proactively check if recent coding sessions violated established playbook rules. Helps catch regressions and reinforces good practices.

Implementation flow:
1. Load current playbook (active bullets only)
2. Get recent sessions:
   - Call `cass timeline --days N --json`
   - Filter by workspace if specified
   
3. Scan each session for violations:
   - For each session:
     - Export/summarize session content
     - For each playbook bullet:
       - LLM check: did this session violate this rule?
       - If yes: record violation with evidence
   
4. Aggregate violations:
   - Group by severity (derived from bullet maturity)
   - Sort by session timestamp
   
5. Format and display:
   - If --json: output AuditResult as JSON
   - Else: format human-readable report
   - Include actionable recommendations

Example output:
```
AUDIT RESULTS (scanned 15 sessions)

VIOLATIONS FOUND: 2

1. [HIGH] Rule b-abc123: "Always run tests before committing"
   Session: ~/.claude/.../session-001.jsonl
   Evidence: Commit made without test run visible

2. [MEDIUM] Rule b-def456: "Use TypeScript strict mode"
   Session: ~/.cursor/.../session-xyz
   Evidence: tsconfig.json has strict: false
```

This helps maintain playbook adherence over time.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:50:59.73258-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:50:59.73258-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-gssx","title":"[Semantic] warmupEmbeddings - Pre-load model on init","description":"Pre-load embedding model during initialization for better UX.

Implementation:
```typescript
async function warmupEmbeddings(): Promise<boolean> {
  try {
    console.log("Warming up embedding model...");
    const embed = await getEmbedder();
    
    // Run test embedding to fully load model
    await embed("test", { pooling: "mean", normalize: true });
    
    console.log("Embedding model ready");
    return true;
  } catch (error) {
    console.warn("Could not warm up embeddings:", error.message);
    return false;
  }
}
```

Why warm up:
- First embedding is slow (~500ms model load)
- Subsequent embeddings are fast (~3ms)
- Better UX to load during init than during first query

When to warm up:
- During cass-memory init
- Background async (non-blocking)
- Before first context/stats command
- After config change

Performance:
- Warmup time: ~500ms (first time)
- Warmup time: ~50ms (cached model)
- Benefit: First query is instant

Usage in CLI:
```typescript
// In main.ts
async function init() {
  const config = await loadConfig();
  
  if (config.semanticSearchEnabled) {
    // Do not await - let it warm up in background
    warmupEmbeddings().catch(() => {
      config.semanticSearchEnabled = false;
    });
  }
}
```

Alternative: Lazy warmup
- Do not warm up on init
- Warm up on first semantic operation
- Trade-off: Slower first command, faster startup

Config option:
```yaml
semantic:
  warmupOnInit: true  # Pre-load model
  # vs
  warmupOnInit: false  # Lazy load on first use
```","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:49.949535-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:55.881019-05:00","comments":[{"id":34,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-gssx","author":"jemanuel","text":"Pre-load embedding model during initialization for better UX.

    return false;
  }
}
```

Why warm up:
- First embedding is slow (~500ms model load)
- Subsequent embeddings are fast (~3ms)
- Better UX to load during init than during first query

When to warm up:
- During cass-memory init

- Before first context/stats command
- After config change

Performance:
- Warmup time: ~500ms (first time)
- Warmup time: ~50ms (cached model)
- Benefit: First query is instant

Usage in CLI:
```typescript

  const config = await loadConfig();

    // Don't await - let it warm up in background

```

Alternative: Lazy warmup
- Don't warm up on init

Config option:
```yaml

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-gwr","title":"[Validator] EvidenceGateResult type definition","description":"Define the EvidenceGateResult type for pre-LLM filter results.

TypeScript Interface:
```typescript
interface EvidenceGateResult {
  passed: boolean;           // Whether the rule passes the evidence gate
  reason: string;            // Human-readable explanation
  suggestedState: "draft" | "active";  // Recommended lifecycle state
  sessionCount: number;      // Number of unique sessions containing evidence
  successCount: number;      // Sessions that ended successfully
  failureCount: number;      // Sessions that failed
}
```

WHY THIS MATTERS:
- Pre-LLM filter result - cheap check before expensive LLM call
- Provides transparency into validation decision-making
- Includes correlation data from diary entries
- Guides lifecycle state assignment (draft vs active)

USAGE:
- Returned by evidenceCountGate() function
- Consumed by validateDelta() to determine next steps
- Used in validation reports for audit trail

DEPENDENCIES:
- None (pure type definition)

LOCATION:
- src/types/validator.ts (or similar)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:50.603554-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:53.616177-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-gyur","title":"[Semantic] embedText - Generate embedding vector","description":"Generate 384-dimensional embedding vector from text.

Implementation:
```typescript
async function embedText(text: string): Promise<number[]> {
  const embed = await getEmbedder();
  const result = await embed(text, {
    pooling: "mean",      // Average all token embeddings
    normalize: true        // Unit vector for cosine similarity
  });
  return Array.from(result.data);  // Float32Array → number[]
}
```

Input:
- Any text string (bullet content, task description, etc.)
- Automatically tokenized and truncated if too long
- Max tokens: 512 (model limit)

Output:
- 384-dimensional number array
- Normalized to unit vector (L2 norm = 1)
- Values typically in [-1, 1]

Performance:
- ~3ms per embedding (after model load)
- Can batch for efficiency

Use cases:
- Bullet content embedding
- Task query embedding
- Semantic search
- Duplicate detection","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:28.793899-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:57.309122-05:00","comments":[{"id":23,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-gyur","author":"jemanuel","text":"Generate 384-dimensional embedding vector from text.

Implementation:

    pooling: "mean",      // Average all token embeddings
    normalize: true        // Unit vector for cosine similarity
  });
  return Array.from(result.data);  // Float32Array → number[]
}
```

Input:

@@ -334,7 +334,7 @@
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-y9k","title":"[Foundation] tsconfig.json with strict TypeScript settings","description":"Create tsconfig.json with strict TypeScript configuration for type safety and ESM module support.

## Context
TypeScript configuration is critical for ensuring type safety, proper module resolution, and IDE support throughout the CASS Memory System codebase. The configuration must support ESM, strict type checking, and clean import paths.

## Technical Specification

### Compiler Options:
- target: ESNext - Use latest JavaScript features
- module: ESNext - ES module output
- moduleResolution: bundler - Modern resolution for Bun
- strict: true - Enable all strict type checking options
- esModuleInterop: true - Better compatibility with CommonJS modules
- skipLibCheck: true - Skip type checking of declaration files for faster builds
- resolveJsonModule: true - Allow importing JSON files
- allowSyntheticDefaultImports: true - Allow default imports from modules with no default export
- forceConsistentCasingInFileNames: true - Ensure consistent file name casing
- noImplicitAny: true - Disallow implicit any types
- strictNullChecks: true - Strict null checking
- strictFunctionTypes: true - Strict function type checking
- noUnusedLocals: true - Report errors on unused local variables
- noUnusedParameters: true - Report errors on unused parameters
- noImplicitReturns: true - Report error when not all code paths return a value
- noFallthroughCasesInSwitch: true - Report errors for fallthrough cases in switch

### Path Mapping:
- paths: { @/*: [./src/*] } - Clean imports like import { Playbook } from '@/types'

### Include/Exclude:
- include: [src/**/*] - Include all source files
- exclude: [node_modules, dist] - Exclude dependencies and build output

### Output:
- outDir: ./dist - Output directory for compiled files
- declaration: true - Generate .d.ts files for library use
- declarationMap: true - Generate sourcemaps for declarations
- sourceMap: true - Generate sourcemaps for debugging

## Reasoning

### Why Strict Mode:
1. Catch Bugs Early: Strict null checks prevent null/undefined errors at runtime
2. Better Refactoring: Type errors guide you when making changes
3. IDE Support: Better autocomplete and IntelliSense
4. LLM Integration: Type safety is crucial when parsing LLM responses

### Why ESNext Target:
1. Bun Support: Bun natively supports modern JavaScript
2. Smaller Polyfills: No need to transpile to ES5
3. Modern Features: Use async/await, optional chaining, etc.

### Why Bundler Module Resolution:
1. Modern Standard: Aligns with modern bundlers and runtimes
2. Better Imports: Supports package.json exports field
3. Bun Compatibility: Matches Bun's module resolution algorithm

### Why Path Mapping:
1. Clean Imports: @/types instead of ../../../types
2. Refactor-Friendly: Moving files doesn't break imports
3. Readability: Clear namespace separation

## Acceptance Criteria

- tsconfig.json exists in project root
- strict: true is enabled
- target: ESNext is set
- module: ESNext is set
- moduleResolution: bundler is set
- Path mapping configured for @/* -> ./src/*
- include/exclude properly configured
- outDir set to ./dist
- bun run typecheck runs without errors on initial project
- IDE shows proper IntelliSense for imports
- No implicit any warnings in editor

## How This Serves the Universal Agent Memory System

TypeScript configuration is the quality gate for the entire codebase:

1. Type Safety: Prevents runtime errors when parsing LLM responses, file operations, and config management
2. Developer Experience: IntelliSense and autocomplete make development faster and more reliable
3. Maintainability: Strong types serve as living documentation for the codebase
4. Refactoring Confidence: Type errors guide safe refactoring as the system evolves
5. LLM Output Validation: Zod schemas combined with TypeScript types ensure structured outputs match expectations

Without strict TypeScript, the system would be prone to runtime errors, especially when dealing with dynamic LLM responses and user configurations.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:44.254164-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:44.254164-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-yagh","title":"[Critical] User-Friendly Error Messages","description":"# User-Friendly Error Messages

## The Problem
Developer tools often have cryptic error messages:
- "Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory"
- "Unexpected token '<'"
- "Connection refused"

Users don't know what to do. They abandon the tool.

## The Solution
Every error should have:
1. What happened (clear description)
2. Why it happened (if known)
3. How to fix it (actionable)

### Error Format:
```typescript
interface UserError {
  code: string;          // "CASS_NOT_FOUND"
  message: string;       // "cass is not installed"
  details?: string;      // "cass is required for session search"
  fix: string;           // "Install cass: brew install cass"
  docs?: string;         // "https://docs.example.com/install"
}
```

### Example Errors:

**cass not found:**
```
❌ cass-memory: cass is not installed

cass is required for searching your agent session history.

To fix:
  brew install cass
  # or
  cargo install cass

Then run: cass index --full

Docs: https://github.com/user/cass
```

**LLM API key missing:**
```
❌ cass-memory: No LLM provider configured

cass-memory needs an LLM for advanced features.

To fix (choose one):
  export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-key
  export OPENAI_API_KEY=your-key

Or run in local-only mode (limited features):
  cass context "task" --no-llm
```

**Playbook corrupt:**
```
❌ cass-memory: Playbook file is corrupt

The playbook at ~/.cass-memory/playbook.json has invalid JSON.

To fix:
  1. Check for backup: ls ~/.cass-memory/*.backup
  2. If backup exists: cp playbook.json.backup playbook.json
  3. Otherwise: cass status --fix (creates empty playbook)

The corrupted file has been saved to: playbook.json.corrupt
```

**Network timeout:**
```
❌ cass-memory: LLM request timed out

The LLM provider (anthropic) did not respond in time.

Possible causes:
  - Network connectivity issues
  - LLM provider outage
  - Rate limiting

To continue without LLM:
  cass reflect --no-llm

To check provider status:
  https://status.anthropic.com
```

### Implementation:
```typescript
function formatError(error: UserError): string {
  let output = `❌ cass-memory: ${error.message}\n\n`;
  
  if (error.details) {
    output += `${error.details}\n\n`;
  }
  
  output += `To fix:\n  ${error.fix.split('\n').join('\n  ')}\n`;
  
  if (error.docs) {
    output += `\nDocs: ${error.docs}\n`;
  }
  
  return output;
}
```

### Error Catalog:
Create a central error catalog with all known errors:

```typescript
const ERRORS = {
  CASS_NOT_FOUND: {
    code: "CASS_NOT_FOUND",
    message: "cass is not installed",
    details: "cass is required for searching your agent session history.",
    fix: "brew install cass\n# or\ncargo install cass\n\nThen run: cass index --full",
    docs: "https://github.com/user/cass"
  },
  // ... more errors
};
```

## Why This Is Critical
- First error experience shapes perception of tool
- Actionable errors = users can self-serve
- Good errors reduce support burden
- Bad errors = users give up","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:16:46.56704-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:16:46.56704-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ydz","title":"[LLM] generateSearchQueries - Task-based query generation","description":"Function signature:
async function generateSearchQueries(task: string, config: Config): Promise<string[]>

Schema:
{
  queries: z.array(z.string()).max(5).describe('3-5 diverse search queries')
}

Prompt:
'Given this task: {task}

Generate 3-5 diverse search queries to find relevant information in cass:
- Similar problems encountered before
- Related frameworks or tools
- Relevant patterns or best practices
- Error messages or debugging approaches

Make queries specific enough to be useful but broad enough to match variations.'

Implementation:
1. Build prompt with task
2. Call generateObject with:
   - schema: queries array
   - temperature: 0.5
3. Return queries array

Used by: Generator phase to expand task into multiple cass search angles.

Example: Task 'add authentication' → queries: ['JWT implementation', 'session management', 'password hashing', 'OAuth setup']","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:02.92747-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:46.001476-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-ygng","title":"[Critical] Implicit Feedback - Auto-Mark from Session Outcomes","description":"# Implicit Feedback - Auto-Mark from Session Outcomes

## The Problem
Current plan requires manual `cass mark helpful/harmful <rule>` commands.
Users won't do this. They'll use the tool, benefit from rules, but never give feedback.
Without feedback, the confidence system is useless.

## The Solution
Infer feedback automatically from observable signals:

### Signal Sources:
1. **Session Success/Failure**: Did the task complete? Errors? Retries?
2. **Time to Completion**: Fast = rules helped. Slow = rules didn't help or were wrong.
3. **Pattern Matching**: Did the session use patterns matching rules we showed?
4. **Explicit Signals**: User said "that worked!" or "that was wrong"

### Implementation:
```typescript
interface SessionOutcome {
  sessionPath: string;
  startTime: Date;
  endTime: Date;
  rulesShown: string[];  // Rule IDs shown in context
  signals: {
    completedSuccessfully: boolean;  // No error exit
    hadRetries: boolean;  // Multiple attempts at same thing
    userSentiment: "positive" | "negative" | "neutral" | null;
    taskDuration: number;  // Seconds
    errorCount: number;
  };
}

async function inferFeedback(outcome: SessionOutcome): Promise<FeedbackEvent[]> {
  const feedback: FeedbackEvent[] = [];
  
  for (const ruleId of outcome.rulesShown) {
    let implicit: FeedbackStrength | null = null;
    
    // Strong positive: Quick success with no errors
    if (outcome.signals.completedSuccessfully && 
        !outcome.signals.hadRetries &&
        outcome.signals.taskDuration < FAST_THRESHOLD) {
      implicit = "helpful";
    }
    
    // Strong negative: Errors or retries on tasks using this rule
    if (outcome.signals.errorCount > 2 || outcome.signals.hadRetries) {
      implicit = "not_helpful";
    }
    
    // Explicit overrides implicit
    if (outcome.signals.userSentiment === "positive") {
      implicit = "very_helpful";
    } else if (outcome.signals.userSentiment === "negative") {
      implicit = "harmful";
    }
    
    if (implicit) {
      feedback.push({
        bulletId: ruleId,
        strength: implicit,
        source: "implicit",
        sessionId: outcome.sessionPath,
        timestamp: new Date()
      });
    }
  }
  
  return feedback;
}
```

### Sentiment Detection (Lightweight):
```typescript
const POSITIVE_PATTERNS = [
  /that worked/i, /perfect/i, /thanks/i, /great/i,
  /exactly what I needed/i, /solved it/i
];

const NEGATIVE_PATTERNS = [
  /that('s| is) wrong/i, /doesn't work/i, /broke/i,
  /not what I wanted/i, /try again/i, /undo/i
];

function detectSentiment(sessionText: string): "positive" | "negative" | "neutral" {
  const positiveCount = POSITIVE_PATTERNS.filter(p => p.test(sessionText)).length;
  const negativeCount = NEGATIVE_PATTERNS.filter(p => p.test(sessionText)).length;
  
  if (positiveCount > negativeCount) return "positive";
  if (negativeCount > positiveCount) return "negative";
  return "neutral";
}
```

### Feedback Weighting:
```typescript
const FEEDBACK_WEIGHTS = {
  // Explicit feedback (user ran command)
  "explicit_helpful": 2.0,
  "explicit_harmful": -3.0,
  
  // Implicit from session outcome
  "implicit_helpful": 0.5,
  "implicit_not_helpful": -0.3,
  
  // Implicit from sentiment
  "sentiment_positive": 0.3,
  "sentiment_negative": -0.5
};
```

### Post-Session Hook:
```typescript
// Runs automatically after cass detects session end
async function onSessionEnd(sessionPath: string): Promise<void> {
  // 1. Get rules that were shown for this session
  const contextLog = await loadContextLog(sessionPath);
  if (!contextLog?.rulesShown) return;  // Context wasn't used
  
  // 2. Analyze session outcome
  const outcome = await analyzeSession(sessionPath);
  outcome.rulesShown = contextLog.rulesShown;
  
  // 3. Infer and record feedback
  const feedback = await inferFeedback(outcome);
  for (const f of feedback) {
    await recordFeedbackEvent(f);
  }
  
  // 4. Update confidence scores
  await updateConfidenceScores(feedback);
}
```

## Why This Is Critical
- Manual feedback has ~0% adoption rate
- Without feedback, confidence scores never update
- System can't learn what works vs what doesn't
- Implicit feedback is "good enough" for most cases
- Can always add explicit feedback later for edge cases

## Trade-offs
- False positives/negatives possible (mitigated by low weights)
- Privacy: Analyzing session content (already in cass, so OK)
- Complexity: Need to track which rules were shown","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:08:16.860446-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:12:38.174224-05:00","comments":[{"id":38,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-ygng","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Problem: Users Won't Provide Manual Feedback
Nobody will consistently run `cass-memory mark b-xyz --helpful` for every useful bullet.

## Solution: Implicit Feedback Signals
1. Session outcome correlation (bullet shown + success = implicit helpful)
2. Audit-based feedback (violations auto-mark harmful)
3. Agent integration (batch marking)
4. Interactive batch marking mode

## Acceptance Criteria
- Track which bullets shown in each context call
- Correlate with session outcomes
- Implement implicit +0.5 helpful for success correlation
- Add --batch-helpful and --interactive flags

+{"id":"cass_memory_system-ygng","title":"[Critical] Implicit Feedback - Auto-Mark from Session Outcomes","description":"# Implicit Feedback - Auto-Mark from Session Outcomes

## The Problem
Current plan requires manual `cass mark helpful/harmful <rule>` commands.
Users won't do this. They'll use the tool, benefit from rules, but never give feedback.
Without feedback, the confidence system is useless.

## The Solution
Infer feedback automatically from observable signals:

### Signal Sources:
1. **Session Success/Failure**: Did the task complete? Errors? Retries?
2. **Time to Completion**: Fast = rules helped. Slow = rules didn't help or were wrong.
3. **Pattern Matching**: Did the session use patterns matching rules we showed?
4. **Explicit Signals**: User said "that worked!" or "that was wrong"

### Implementation:
```typescript
interface SessionOutcome {
  sessionPath: string;
  startTime: Date;
  endTime: Date;
  rulesShown: string[];  // Rule IDs shown in context
  signals: {
    completedSuccessfully: boolean;  // No error exit
    hadRetries: boolean;  // Multiple attempts at same thing
    userSentiment: "positive" | "negative" | "neutral" | null;
    taskDuration: number;  // Seconds
    errorCount: number;
  };
}

async function inferFeedback(outcome: SessionOutcome): Promise<FeedbackEvent[]> {
  const feedback: FeedbackEvent[] = [];
  
  for (const ruleId of outcome.rulesShown) {
    let implicit: FeedbackStrength | null = null;
    
    // Strong positive: Quick success with no errors
    if (outcome.signals.completedSuccessfully && 
        !outcome.signals.hadRetries &&
        outcome.signals.taskDuration < FAST_THRESHOLD) {
      implicit = "helpful";
    }
    
    // Strong negative: Errors or retries on tasks using this rule
    if (outcome.signals.errorCount > 2 || outcome.signals.hadRetries) {
      implicit = "not_helpful";
    }
    
    // Explicit overrides implicit
    if (outcome.signals.userSentiment === "positive") {
      implicit = "very_helpful";
    } else if (outcome.signals.userSentiment === "negative") {
      implicit = "harmful";
    }
    
    if (implicit) {
      feedback.push({
        bulletId: ruleId,
        strength: implicit,
        source: "implicit",
        sessionId: outcome.sessionPath,
        timestamp: new Date()
      });
    }
  }
  
  return feedback;
}
```

### Sentiment Detection (Lightweight):
```typescript
const POSITIVE_PATTERNS = [
  /that worked/i, /perfect/i, /thanks/i, /great/i,
  /exactly what I needed/i, /solved it/i
];

const NEGATIVE_PATTERNS = [
  /that('s| is) wrong/i, /doesn't work/i, /broke/i,
  /not what I wanted/i, /try again/i, /undo/i
];

function detectSentiment(sessionText: string): "positive" | "negative" | "neutral" {
  const positiveCount = POSITIVE_PATTERNS.filter(p => p.test(sessionText)).length;
  const negativeCount = NEGATIVE_PATTERNS.filter(p => p.test(sessionText)).length;
  
  if (positiveCount > negativeCount) return "positive";
  if (negativeCount > positiveCount) return "negative";
  return "neutral";
}
```

### Feedback Weighting:
```typescript
const FEEDBACK_WEIGHTS = {
  // Explicit feedback (user ran command)
  "explicit_helpful": 2.0,
  "explicit_harmful": -3.0,
  
  // Implicit from session outcome
  "implicit_helpful": 0.5,
  "implicit_not_helpful": -0.3,
  
  // Implicit from sentiment
  "sentiment_positive": 0.3,
  "sentiment_negative": -0.5
};
```

### Post-Session Hook:
```typescript
// Runs automatically after cass detects session end
async function onSessionEnd(sessionPath: string): Promise<void> {
  // 1. Get rules that were shown for this session
  const contextLog = await loadContextLog(sessionPath);
  if (!contextLog?.rulesShown) return;  // Context wasn't used
  
  // 2. Analyze session outcome
  const outcome = await analyzeSession(sessionPath);
  outcome.rulesShown = contextLog.rulesShown;
  
  // 3. Infer and record feedback
  const feedback = await inferFeedback(outcome);
  for (const f of feedback) {
    await recordFeedbackEvent(f);
  }
  
  // 4. Update confidence scores
  await updateConfidenceScores(feedback);
}
```

## Why This Is Critical
- Manual feedback has ~0% adoption rate
- Without feedback, confidence scores never update
- System can't learn what works vs what doesn't
- Implicit feedback is "good enough" for most cases
- Can always add explicit feedback later for edge cases

## Trade-offs
- False positives/negatives possible (mitigated by low weights)
- Privacy: Analyzing session content (already in cass, so OK)
- Complexity: Need to track which rules were shown","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:08:16.860446-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:12:38.174224-05:00","comments":[{"id":38,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-ygng","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Problem: Users Won't Provide Manual Feedback

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ypmh","title":"[ErrorHandling] validateAndSanitizeInput - Input validation","description":"Validate and sanitize user input to prevent errors and security issues.

Function signature:
function validateAndSanitizeInput(
  type: 'bulletId' | 'sessionPath' | 'task' | 'category',
  value: string
): string

Validation rules:
- bulletId: Must match pattern (e.g., 'B001', alphanumeric)
- sessionPath: Valid file path, resolve relative to absolute
- task: Non-empty, max length, no control characters
- category: Must be in allowed list or match pattern

Sanitization:
- Trim whitespace
- Normalize unicode
- Remove control characters
- Resolve paths to absolute

Throws descriptive error with hint if invalid:
- What was invalid
- What format is expected
- Example of valid input

Returns sanitized value on success.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:27.710962-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:27.710962-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-yqds","title":"[Tracking] atomicWrite - Safe file updates","description":"Purpose: Prevent file corruption on crashes/interruptions during writes

Function signature:
```typescript
async function atomicWrite(
  filePath: string,
  content: string
): Promise<void>
```

Implementation steps:
1. Generate temp file path: {filePath}.tmp
2. Write content to temp file
3. Call fsync() on temp file descriptor to ensure data on disk
4. Close temp file
5. Rename temp file to final path (atomic operation on POSIX)
6. On error: cleanup temp file and rethrow

Platform considerations:
- POSIX (macOS/Linux): rename() is atomic
- Windows: May need MoveFileEx with MOVEFILE_REPLACE_EXISTING

Error handling:
```typescript
try {
  await fs.writeFile(tmpPath, content);
  const fd = await fs.open(tmpPath, 'r+');
  await fd.sync();
  await fd.close();
  await fs.rename(tmpPath, filePath);
} catch (err) {
  await fs.unlink(tmpPath).catch(() => {});
  throw err;
}
```

Used for: Playbooks, config files, processed logs, all critical data files","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:30.763028-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:30.763028-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-yue","title":"[Diary] findDiaryBySession - Lookup by session path","description":"Find diary entry by session path, used for evidence correlation.

Function Signature:
async function findDiaryBySession(
  sessionPath: string,
  config: Config
): Promise<DiaryEntry | null>

Process:
1. List all files in diary directory
2. Filter for JSON files matching diary filename pattern
3. For each diary file:
   a. Load diary using loadDiaryEntry
   b. Check if diary.sessionPath === sessionPath
   c. Return diary if match found
4. Return null if no match found

Optimization:
- Early exit on first match (no need to scan all)
- Cache recently loaded diaries in memory
- Use filename hash to skip unlikely matches

Key Features:
- Exact path matching
- Graceful null return on no match
- Error resilient (skips corrupted diaries)
- Performance optimized for large diary collections

Use Cases:
- Validator looking up evidence for rule
- Checking if diary already exists for session
- Correlating playbook rules with diary insights
- Evidence chain construction

Example:
const session = '~/.claude/projects/myapp/session-123.jsonl';
const diary = await findDiaryBySession(session, config);
if (diary) {
  console.log('Session outcome:', diary.status);
  console.log('Key learnings:', diary.keyLearnings);
}

Performance:
- O(n) worst case where n = number of diaries
- O(1) best case with early exit
- Consider indexing for large collections (future enhancement)

Output:
Returns DiaryEntry if found, null otherwise.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:22.156714-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:34.628882-05:00"}
